sections.title: Talking to Servius again before going to meet with Queen Zyanyi

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Queen is waiting, my child. Let us go and speak with her.

sections.title: Talking to Servius again after meeting with Queen Zyanyi

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. I wish you luck at the Twilight Temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. We'll have Itzla back soon. I promise.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: You have the dedication of a true servant of Ralos. Keep safe, my child.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You have a job to do. Return to the Twilight Temple and uncover any information you may have missed when you were last there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go right away.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Good. Don't let me down.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. Your arrival is well timed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Yes. The Queen would like to speak with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Indeed. She is waiting for you upstairs. I will go on ahead and let her know you are here.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: I am told you were with my son when he was captured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You followed along with this plan of his, knowing that in doing so, you were supporting him in disobeying an order from his Queen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You do not strike me as a fool, so why do you play the part of one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can fix this. I promise you, I will find Itzla and I will rescue him.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Why should I trust you? Because my son did? Tell me, which recent event was it that suggested to you that he was of sound judgement?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: With respect, my Queen, were it not for [player name] here, I would have died at the hands of the Emissaries. I believe [he has/she has/they have] earned our trust.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Trust is like a blade. If not maintained, it fails when you need it most.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Then let us give our iknami here the opportunity to regain our trust.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You seem to have made quite the impression, outsider. Both Servius and my son have taken a liking to you.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Prove them right. Rectify this situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will. Where should I begin?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: We need to avoid outright aggression. The safety of my son cannot be risked. I understand you were able to infiltrate the Emissaries and gain access to their temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then do so again. Gather any information you can on what they are doing with my son, then return to me here. Do not give me a reason to regret this decision.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't let you down. You have my word.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Good luck, my child. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need to do something else first, but I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Don't take too long. The Queen doesn't like to be kept waiting.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Hey! You don't have permission to go in there!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: ... and with the guidance of Ralos and Ranul, who stand beside us in our fight, we will be victorious!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: I know recent setbacks have you questioning. I know you have concerns. You need not whisper them in the shadows, for you are free to question. More than that, you are encouraged to question!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: After all, was it not your questions that brought you here? Did you not dare to ask them while others shied away in fear?

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Remember this: We are the doubters! We are the outcasts! Society shunned us for it, but only those who question everything will ever find the truth.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: And that truth? That is what we fight for. That is what will bring about the Final Dawn!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Let me now assure you, these setbacks have done nothing to stop us! The road to Mokhaiotl is clearer than ever! We have no need for the Teokan any more!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Bring out the prisoner!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: See how the rulers of this kingdom are powerless against us. Itzla Arkan, heir to the throne of Varlamore, is now our prisoner!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You love it, don't you? Standing on a stage spouting nonsense to a bunch of fanatics! It means nothing, Metzli!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: People like you always fear the truth. Do you know why? Because without your lies, all that you do, all that you live for, is meaningless.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: But your fear, it can now end. Here, you finally have a purpose. Here, you can finally serve Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Do you not see? The blood of the Teokan runs through your veins. You are now the key to the Final Dawn!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You want my blood? Take it! Just make sure you take it all! At least that way I won't have to listen to the garbage you spew any more!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Listen to the anger in his voice. It is a poor attempt to mask his fear. We need not fear though, for our purpose is clear.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Go now, for time is short, and there is much left to do. It will soon be time to deliver Twilight's Promise. It will soon be time for the Final Dawn!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... They brought Itzla in from downstairs...

sections.title: Talking to an Emissary Ascended, Chosen, or Acolyte

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: The people of this world do not have the strength to do what must be done. Ximoua will only come to be if we make it so.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Greetings, friend. Our time grows ever near. Ralos and Ranul will unite us all as one, and we will create a new world.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: It is an honour to serve the Augur. She will lead us to Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: The Final Dawn approaches, friend. We will deliver Twilight's Promise.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: I can't let you down there without the passphrase.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [phrase 1]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [phrase 2]?

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Nice try. Only those worthy to know the passphrase may enter.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Correct!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that was it?

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: That's right. Go right ahead.

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: This area is off limits to you!

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: This is the restricted section!

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: You're not allowed to be here!

sections.title: Searching the bed with a full inventory
Searching the drawer with a full inventory
Using the canvas piece on the drawing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah...

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: What is this? No one is allowed in here!

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: Give me that!

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: There's only one outcome for betraying the Augur...

lines.speaker: Emissary Enforcer
lines.text: Death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be something useful here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh...

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: ... not to mention what happens when the Queen sends her forces to recover the Prince.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: The Queen will not dare put her son in danger. Your worries are unfounded. Besides, I've already made arrangements for him to be moved elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Unfounded? Do you not see how risky this plan is? We should have stuck with Servius rather than revealing ourselves.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: And if the opportunity presents itself, Servius will still make for a valid option. The trick here isn't to have one plan, it's to have many. We have enough options available that one of them will bear fruit.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: It won't matter how many options we have if we don't live long enough to see them through. You keep gambling with our future here!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: You should have more faith, Furia. Look at your brother. He's a true believer now.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: A fool is what he is.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Good job we have many uses for fools then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was close...

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. The Queen has given you a job to do. I suggest you get going. Keep safe out there.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Adventurer, you should head to this cultist property near the western bank. Captain Vibia will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head over at once.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Good. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You've returned. I trust that means you have news for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do. Itzla was being held at the temple, but they've now moved him elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, but I did also find out that they have some sort of plan for him. They called him the key to the Final Dawn. Something to do with him having Servius' blood.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: My blood? Do they wish to trigger the Gathering?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Gathering? What's the Gathering?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: What it is isn't important right now. Do not lose focus on the matter at hand. If you don't know where they took my son, then how do you intend to find out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I discovered that Forebearer Janus, a senior member of the Emissaries, is living right here in Fortis in a cult safe house. I was thinking we could capture him and find out what he knows.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Where is this safe house?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Directly opposite a bank apparently.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Well, there are no houses opposite the eastern bank, so it must be the one looking over the western bank.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then let's not waste any time. Adventurer, I will dispatch Captain Vibia of the Knights of Varlamore to this property. You will meet her there. Secure the property with her and capture this Forebearer Janus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head over there at once.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Good. May the sun light your way.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the front door

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: We're wasting time here. You need to find a way into this house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Good, you're here. Shall we get started?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess so...

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: I've been observing the property and it doesn't seem anyone is home. At least, not any people...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: People? Meaning...

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Dogs. There's one outside and at least one more in the house. I don't expect them to take kindly to intruders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's our plan?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: We will secure the house and prepare a trap to capture this cultist leader upon their return. As we don't know exactly when that will be, I suggest we move quickly.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: First up, we need a way inside. I will keep watch out here while you find us that way in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to work.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Vibia after giving the dog bones

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: That cultist could be back at any time. What's the hold up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a way into the house, but there's a locked door I need to get past. I'll have it open soon.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Good. Best you get back to it then.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Good work. Now that the property is secure, we need to prepare for the return of this Forebearer Janus. I trust you brought supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... What kind of supplies?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: First, we'll need a way to restrain him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I don't think I have anything suitable on me.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Well, what about something to knock him out with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I have anything for that either. Shall I head out and gather some things?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: No. This cultist could be back at any moment and we need to make sure we're here when he does. Let's have a look around and see if there's anything in the house we can use instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're a Knight of Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: I am. What of it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That makes Itzla your boss, right?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: He is the leader of the Knights of Varlamore, though I am currently filling that role in his absense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of this whole situation with him?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: I think we have a job to do, and I think we should get on with it rather than making small talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get searching.

sections.title: Using a bucket on the potted fan
Using the empty coin purse on the potted fan
Using a knife on the potted fan
Using a knife on the potted fan again
Using the branch on the potted fan
Using the empty coin purse on the branch

Using a bucket of sand on the plant pot
Using the empty coin purse on the plant pot
Using the sandy coin purse on the plant pot
Using the branch on the sandy coin purse
Using a rope on Captain Vibia

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reckon we can use this?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Hmm... I guess we could use this to restrain him, but I reckon we'll instead want something that can cover his head as well.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: No. I think that sack you gave me will be better for restraining him with.

sections.title: Using any blackjack on Captain Vibia

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reckon we can use this?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: That thing looks brutal! We don't want to kill him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean this thing could kill a person?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Quite possibily!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: That thing looks brutal! We don't want to kill him! Maybe you should use that other one you made instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reckon we can use this?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Yes, I think I can use this to restrain him with. I'll keep a hold of it.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Hmm... Not on its own, I don't think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made this from a few things I found.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Perfect! You keep a hold of that and use it to subdue him when the time comes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think we have everything we need now.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: I believe you're right. Now just to wait for him to return home...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, look! I'm a distraction!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Hey! Get this thing off me!

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Whack him!

sections.title: Talking to the Queen again after refusing her offer to travel to Cam Torum

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: [Player name], do you wish to travel with us to Cam Torum?

sections.title: Talking to Servius before leaving for Cam Torum

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. We will soon be heading to Cam Torum. You should speak with the Queen if you wish to join us.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Vibia before leaving for Cam Torum

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: I hear you're heading to Cam Torum. You should not waste time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you not joining us?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: No. I am to head to the Tlati Rainforest and ensure we have suitable defences around the Crypt of Tonali.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makes sense. In that case, good luck.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibial
lines.text: And to you, adventurer. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found the basement key.

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Good. Down there will work nicely for interrogation. You get him ready. I'll fetch the Queen...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: The Queen herself? Such an honour. Don't think for one second that I'm going to tell you anything though. Even death itself doesn't scare me.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Really? I've known a lot of people claim death doesn't scare them. From afar, bravery comes easy, but then the moment finally comes when they stare death in the face...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: That's when they break. If you think you're the exception, you will soon be disappointed.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Ha! You are out of touch, my Queen. I am a herald of the Final Dawn! For us, death is a glory to be celebrated!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then celebrate it you will, but first, you will talk.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Leave me with him. Alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: He talked.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: It seems you were right Servius, the cultists wish to trigger the Gathering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but could someone explain what the Gathering is to me?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: It's the ceremony where a dead Teokan is laid to rest and their successor is chosen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why is it so important to the cultists?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: According to our recently departed iknami here, it's less about the ceremony and more about its location: The Crypt of Tonali, in the Tlati Rainforest. The cult believe the crypt houses the secret path to Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Tlati Rainforest? Just like the nagua said...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Yes, Servius mentioned this nagua you encountered. What did she say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much. She just referred to Mokhaiotl as the Tlati archive.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: We should be weary of trusting the words of a nagua, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Perhaps, though it matters little whether we believe her or not. The cult do believe, and they will stop at nothing to access the crypt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do now?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: The Gathering takes place behind a set of doors within the crypt. These doors can only be opened with the blood of the two Teokan.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: The cult are hoping to substitute Servius' blood with my son's, but it seems they are having issues in doing so. They have apparently chosen to seek help from the Dwarves of Cam Torum with this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why the dwarves?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The crypt is the most holiest of places, built by the Old Ones themselves. However, the doors were a more recent addition, built by the dwarves. This was before relations soured between us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So, to Cam Torum then?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: To Cam Torum. Oh, and [player name]...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You did well here. It seems Itzla was right to trust you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Now, we will depart at once. Are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make my own way there.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Very well, but let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: When I vouched for you back there, [player name], I wasn't expecting you to bring a Teokan with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's here to help.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph. Help with what exactly? You claim there are cultists in our city who want access this crypt of yours, but you haven't said why. What's so special about the crypt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They think it hides a path to Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ah, of course they do. Don't you humans ever tire of your fantasies?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Mokhaiotl is no fantasy!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Why? Have you been yourself?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Of course not! No human can reach Mokhaiotl!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ha! And yet here you are trying to stop some cultists from doing just that!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Their beliefs are false! They follow the words of a lying heathen!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ah, of course! A lying heathen. Just like us here, no doubt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, let's put a stop to this before things get out of hand. Attala, you said you had an idea for where we could start our search?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hmph. As the Highlord said, there are only four dwarves who can grant humans access to Cam Torum. They are myself, the Highlord, Highseer Vandra, and Teumo, Vandra's advisor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you suspect someone?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Not exactly, but of those, Teumo is the most likely candidate. I trust the Highlord and the Highseer, and I know for a fact it wasn't me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's start with Teumo then. Where can we find him?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: His house is the one behind the bank, east of here. Let us meet there.

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: ... and that he should be back soon. Why do you need to see him anyway, Attala? And how come you have a human priest with you?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Believe me, the priest wasn't my choice.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Excuse me!

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Anyway, don't worry yourself, Galna. It's not an urgent matter by any means. It's just a little project that Teumo was working on that needs finishing off.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Speaking of which, do you mind if we take a look around while we wait for him? I imagine he'll have left some things from his project here.

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Oh, I suppose so. Do keep out of the basement though. Teumo doesn't like people going down there.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: He doesn't? Very well...

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. Perhaps there is something here that can be of use to us. You should have a look around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Is there anything we can get for you while we wait, Galna? Given we came barging in on you, it's the least we can do.

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Oh, thank you, but I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Not even a drink?

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Very tempting, but I shouldn't really start drinking at this time...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: If you're sure.

sections.title: Talking to Attala or Galna again

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Thanks again for letting us look around, Galna.

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Not at all! My home is yours. Go wherever you want!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you wouldn't fancy a drink?

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Well, I guess it would be rude to say no...

lines.speaker: Galna
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Attala in the basement

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Teumo must have had a reason for not wanting people down here. Let's find out what it is.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Interesting... Whatever you did caused the fire to go out...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's your take on this Mokhaiotl business?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Well, just so I'm understanding correctly, there's a cult who believe there is a path to the centre of the world?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: And what do they intend to do once they get there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're claiming that two of the cultists are Ralos and Ranul reborn. They believe that once the two of them reach Mokhaiotl, the world will be destroyed and then remade once more.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Of course! What a totally normal thing to believe. Only humans would ever manage to reach this level of silliness.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Do not tar us all with the same brush. Most humans would never believe the nonsense those cultists spread.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: No, you just believe a different nonsense. I must admit, to those of us who live in the real world, it can sometimes be hard to tell the difference.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Anyway, as for my take, your cultists are going to end up disappointed. There is no Mokhaiotl. The only path they will find is one leading to their deaths.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And yet, it isn't just them who have spoken of it.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I encountered a nagua who called Mokhaiotl an archive.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Really? How curious... Hard to know what that might mean though. Nagua are not known for being reliable.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Ah, something we agree on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't think she was telling the truth?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: The truth isn't something that ever seems to matter to your kind. This cult has already made up their minds on what they believe, just as your priest here has as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm not asking them. I'm asking you. What do you believe?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: That Ralos doesn't exist. That Ranul doesn't exist. That Mokhaiotl doesn't exist. It's as simple as that. Now, if that answer satisfies you, shall we get back to work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

sections.title: Talking to Servius in the basement

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. Perhaps there is something here that can be of use to us. You should have a look around and see what you can find.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. It looks like you're making progress. Keep at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If it's okay to ask, what's the deal with you and the Queen?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: How do you mean, my child?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you're Itzla's father and she's his mother, but the two of you don't seem too close.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: We are incredibly close, for we are united in Ralos' light, and in the love we share for Itzla. However, it would be fair to say that our relationship is not a conventional one, especially to an outsider.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did it come about?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: In times of old, the ruler of Varlamore would often unite with a Teokan of Ralos. His light guides us in all things, and a King or Queen will need that guidance more than most.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Before she took the throne, the Queen made the decision to revive that old tradition. I was more than happy to accept.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for explaining.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Do not mention it, my child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

Placing a trapper's tipple on a barrel

Placing an empty beer glass on a barrel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These dwarves sure love their drinks.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Must we wear these nasty things?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: They're called disguises, brother.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: We shouldn't have need to disguise ourselves. We shouldn't be here at all. This dwarf can't be trusted.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: If you have any better suggestions, I'm all ears...

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Ah, there you are. Oh, I'm sorry, am I meant to bow?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: You jest, dwarf, but you should show some respect.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Yes, of course, Your... Holiness? Your Excellency? I'm sorry, I've never had the luck of meeting a god before. What is your preferred title?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Enough! Do not push me, dwarf!

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Why? You are powerless to do anything about it. Without me, you go back to your Augur empty handed. I get the feeling she wouldn't like that.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Well then it's a good thing we do not answer to her.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: No? If that's the case, then why are you doing her bidding? Of all the people she could have sent here, why did she pick her gods reborn?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: The blood of Ralos flows through my veins, dwarf! When we reach Mokhaiotl, you will see for yourself.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Ha! And what will you see? What happens when you reach the apparent centre of the world, only to discover that everything you thought you knew, was wrong?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: You think you're so...

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Calm yourself, brother. Arguing gets us nowhere. Tell us, dwarf, can it be done?

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Yes, I believe so. However, it will not be straightforward.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: I will need you to retrieve something for me. The crypt doors are sealed using the magic of an Old One keystone. I will need one of these keystones for myself if I am to have any success.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: And where will we find one of those?

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: It is a keystone that keeps the nagua imprisoned in Neypotzli. I'm sure it can't be the only one in there.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Then go in and find one for yourself. We do not serve you.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: So you claim, and yet you don't actually have a choice. If you want my help, you'll find me a keystone.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: You will have it. Just make sure you are ready.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Of course! I assume you've already sorted my payment?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Yes, as agreed.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Enjoy it while you can. That money won't mean anything once this is over.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Ah, truth be told, the money is just a bonus. The chance to be the one to show you fools the truth... That, my friends, is the real prize.

lines.speaker: Teumo
lines.text: Anyway, best we all get to work. You have a keystone to find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That didn't sound good. I'd better let Attala and Servius know.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: You're back! Did you find something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did. You were right, Attala. Teumo is the one helping the cultists. He's sent them to Neypotzli to find something he calls an Old One keystone.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: More trouble in Neypotzli? Great! This is just what we need!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: What do we do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Servius, go to Tal Teklan. If we can't stop them from getting that keystone, they'll have everything they need to enter the crypt. You need to make sure the Queen is ready.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I'll gather some guards and see if we can find Teumo. While I do that, [player name], you should go to Neypotzli and find the cultists.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, let's get to it.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Good luck, [player name]. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Zyanyi Arkan. What an unexpected surprise. Your business here must be very important for you to have not sent prior word of your coming.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: I'm afraid the suddenness of our arrival was a necessity, though I'm pleased to see that it didn't prevent you from meeting us here personally, Highlord Katlo.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Well, what kind of ruler would I be if I didn't make sure to treat our honoured allies with the highest of respect?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: How very true.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: I heard about the unfortunate events at the Teomat, but it is good to see the Teokan has made a full recovery. No doubt those responsible met swift justice?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Their days are numbered.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: So they are still at large? How concerning. Still, I'm sure your son and his knights will be doing all they can to rectify that.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Perhaps, Highlord, we should discuss the reason for our visit?

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Yes, of course. What is it that brings you here?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: We have reason to believe that enemies of Varlamore are operating within Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Enemies of Varlamore in Cam Torum? I couldn't even begin to imagine such a thing! Are you sure of this?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Very.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Then what can you tell me of these enemies?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: They are members of the Twilight Emissaries cult. The cult wish to gain access to the Crypt of Tonali, but they require dwarven help to do so.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: I assure you, no cultist would have been granted access to the city. For a start, there are only four of us who even have the ability to grant that access.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: However, it is clear this is a matter of great importance to you. I will ensure that we keep a close eye out for any cultists.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: With respect, Highlord, these cultists are our enemy. I would ask that you let us find and deal with them personally.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: I understand, and I would very much like to help you. However, a human investigation in Cam Torum would not be looked upon kindly by the citizens here.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: If I may, Highlord, the human travelling with the Queen here is a friend to us. They are the one who helped us deal with the issues in the ruins beneath the city.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: If it is this human who is to be conducting the investigations here, I am sure they will act with discretion.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: You are happy to vouch for them, Attala?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Very well. In that case, my good friends, I grant you permission to search for these cultists. Attala here will join you. Do let her know if there's anything at all that you need.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Thank you, Highlord.

lines.speaker: Highlord Katlo
lines.text: Do not mention it. Dealing with Varlamorians is always such a pleasure. Now, I'm afraid I must depart, but I wish you good luck.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I will wait for you in the market, [player name]. I have some ideas for where you might begin your search.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: So you've worked with the dwarves here before? You are full of surprises, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do my best.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Clearly. Now, time is of the essence here. Use the trust you have built with the dwarves to find and stop the cultists. Once you have done so, report to me at Tal Teklan in the Tlati Rainforest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not staying?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: No. My presence here will be a distraction, and I must ensure that the defences are ready at the Crypt of Tonali. However, I trust you will not let me down.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: I will stay as well. It is possible my knowledge will come in useful during the investigation.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Nonsense. Need I remind you that you are still a target for the cultists? You will return to the palace.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Emissaries hide everywhere. I am no safer at the palace than I am here.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Fine. But I will leave a bodyguard with you.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: You should not waste the Kualti on me.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: No I shouldn't, and yet here we are. Now, let us get to work. Good luck to you both, and may the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Thank you, my Queen. Come, [player name], let us meet with this dwarf in the market.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes, iknil?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does this fragment work?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It resonates with the magic of the other keystone. It will guide you to it. You must go there and find the interlopers. They must be removed from Neypotzli.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Good luck, iknil.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Welcome back, iknil. You have timed your arrival here well, for Neypotzli is in danger once more. There are interlopers present who have little regard for the dangers posed by the Moons of Peril.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's exactly why I'm here. I know who these interlopers are and I know what they're after.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes, I suspected that to be the case. Your arrival was too precise to be coincidence. Tell me, what do the interlopers seek?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're after an Old One keystone.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Old One? A curious term. We nagua measure time as the rivers change their paths and as the mountains errode away.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The days of the masters are fresh memories to me, but to you, one who is so short lived, they are days of myth...

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Tell me, why do the interlopers seek the magic of the masters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a crypt in the Tlati Rainforest, but it's sealed using Old One magic. These interlopers are cultists who want to get inside. They think they can do so if they have a keystone of their own.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Provided they have the aptitude, I suspect their beliefs are correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then we need to stop them before they get one.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The icosahedron behind me is a form of keystone, and the interlopers have already tried to tamper with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you stopped them?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes, but the danger remains. There is another keystone here. An earlier form of the icosahedron from when Neypotzli was being built.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: However, the Moons of Peril must remain my priority. I cannot stop the interlopers from trying to obtain it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I can deal with them. I just need to know where they are.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I can give you a fragment of the icosahedron. It resonates with the magic of the other keystone. It will guide you to it. Go to the keystone, and you will no doubt find the interlopers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, but before I go, there's one other thing...

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The reason these cultists want to enter that crypt... It's because they think there's a path to Mokhaiotl inside.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Mokhaiotl?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what it is?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: The masters were always secretive, especially towards the end. They rarely spoke of the world outside this place, but when they did, they only spoke of pain.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: They had already lost so much by the time they summoned me. Their extinction was already guaranteed, yet they kept going all the same.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: This word... Mokhaiotl... I only heard it once, when things neared the end. It was not clear to me what it was, but when they spoke of it... it was the first time I'd heard them speak with hope.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Now, I must return to my focus on the Moons of Peril. Seek me out in the chamber to the south east if you need my guidance. Go with haste, iknil. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Well, look who's here! It's Itzla's little lapdog!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Call me what you want, but the fun's over. You're not going anywhere with that keystone.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Call her in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Metzli!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Ah, the outsider. I see you survived your encounter with the nagua.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It will take more than that to kill me.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Clearly. It's a shame you've chosen to fight against the truth. One such as yourself would have served us well.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Still, no matter. The Final Dawn will soon be upon us, and it will see us all become equal in servitude to our gods.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: You two, guide this one to Mictl. Be sure not to underestimate [him/her/them].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh* Alright, let's get this over with...

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: There you are, [player name]. That nagua told us how to find you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry to say you missed the fun. They got away, Attala. I wasn't able to stop them in time.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: I see. That's not the only bad news, I'm afraid. We searched the whole city, but couldn't find any trace of Teumo. My guess is he's already left to go join those cultists.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning they now have everything they need to enter the crypt.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Sounds like you should head to Tal Teklan and report to your Queen then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before I go, Attala, I'm guessing we shouldn't expect any help from Cam Torum?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: You know as well as I do, [player name], these cultists are fools. There is no world ending threat here, nor any threat to Cam Torum. This is a human problem. It's for humans to deal with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suspected as much.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: If it's any consolation, I hope you don't die. You're one of the few humans that I don't tire of interacting with. Now, I'd better go report to the Highlord.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: [player name], you must go to the Crypt of Tonali and help with the defence. It's beneath the large temple south east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head there right away!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Go with the strength of Ralos, my child!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: [player name]! Thank Ralos you're here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Crypt of Tonali is under attack! The Queen has already gone to help. You should go yourself at once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head there right away!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The crypt is impossible to miss. It's beneath the large temple south east of here. Go, my child! Go with the strength of Ralos!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Hold the line! We must not let them get to those doors!

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Adventurer, about time you showed up! Hold them back! If we let too many defenders fall, nothing will stop those cultists getting through!

sections.title: Upon clearing the waves of cultists

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Watch out! More are coming!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Do not let them through!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: It's time!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Mother...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Get him to safety! Ensure he's unharmed!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Now, let's get after them!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: She's gone, Ennius! Don't you see? We've been abandoned!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: She would not abandon us! We are her gods!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Gods?! My dear brother, how are you this much of a fool?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Don't tell me now that you deny our destiny?! After all that we've done... you now decide that you don't believe?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Now? Ennius, I never believed!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Then what was it all for?!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Yes, Furia, what was it for? You betrayed your people. You betrayed your Queen. Why?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Why? Because it was you who betrayed your people! You destroyed everything we stood for when you decided to open our kingdom! You are not worthy of the throne!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Metzli promised the throne to you instead? Is that it? Seems like that's not going so well.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: You lied to me, Furia!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: I was creating a future for us, brother!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: And now that future is at an end. Come quietly. Metzli has abandoned you. You have nowhere left to run.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: So you can parade us in front of the people before you put us out of our misery? No. This ends here.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Ennius...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: So be it. Kill them both.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Such a waste.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: What happened to Furia?

lines.speaker: Captain Vibia
lines.text: Forgive me, my Queen. She was injured, but she managed to escape down into the caverns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Metzli's still down there as well. This isn't over yet.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: My Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Itzla? What are you doing here? Why aren't you resting?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I heard Metzli got away. I'm here to help.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Absolutely not!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Mother... This is my burden to bear. Let me finish this.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: We've only just got you back, and you want me to send you after her?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You were going to go yourself, even though they need you up here. Why you and not me?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: That's...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: [player name], keep my fool of a son alive, would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Go, the both of you, before I change my mind.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Thank you, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Itzla... [player name]...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Be careful down there. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nagua! I'll deal with them. You go on ahead. Don't worry, I'll catch up.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla at the crypt

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Can you believe this place was down here all this time? We had no idea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You never knew all this was right underneath one of your holy sites?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well... sort of, I suppose. We always knew the crypt was originally built by the Old Ones, but we had no idea how far all of this stretched. It's not like anyone was ever allowed to come down here and check!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, there hasn't been any sign of Metzli or Furia, but they must have come this way. Let's have a look around and see if we can find a way forward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on where to start?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, we seem to have more of these strange teleport tiles here... Maybe we should see where they lead?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We're making progress. Let's keep it up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do now?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, we managed to activate a statue in one of these rooms. My guess is we now need to activate the one in the other.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This room is fascinating...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I think we're making progress, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on what to do?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, we've got another one of those statues, and these altars around the edges also look important. The layout of the room seems quite specific as well. It's almost like a compass, or maybe even a clock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A clock? But there's only eight points.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: True, but maybe the Old Ones used a different kind of clock to us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, this thing we're doing with the altars seems to be working, so I say we just keep doing that.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Of course, iknami! What do you need translating?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oma? I think that means two.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Naui? This is the one I struggle with. It might mean four.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuli? I think that means five.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Chaki? I think that means six.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Koma? I think that means seven.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ueai? I think that means eight.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Makti? I think that means ten.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... I'm not too sure on that one.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Very funny, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! Could you translate another?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I'm done translating for now.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hopefully it helps in some way, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of this essence I found?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... It looks similar to the altars around the room. Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In these urns scattered around.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, I doubt it was left here by accident...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, the teleporter seems to have separated us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, I might still be able to be of use from here. These look like pressure plates. I might be able to use them to help you in some way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea what that 'some way' might be?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm not really sure. Those braziers around the room look like they could be important though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see if I can work something out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea what we should be doing here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well it seems I can use these pressure plates to help you get past the fires. Beyond that, I'm not too sure. Those braziers around the room look like they could be important though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see if I can work something out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We seem to be making progress, [player name]. Keep it up.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm already there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nice work, [player name]! I think you did it!

sections.title: Pulling tree roots with a full inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think we'll find on the other side?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: If all goes well, we find Metzli and Furia and we show them the error of their ways. No doubt they won't be willing to hear reason, so I suspect it will get a touch violent in the process...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But then, once that's done, maybe we go and have a nice drink somewhere. I know I need one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A drink does sound nice, it's just...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That nagua... I keep thinking about what she said about Mokhaiotl being an archive. What does that mean? Could this place be what she was referring to?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... Well, I guess there's only one way to find out... Shall we?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: When you're ready, [player name].

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: They can't be too far ahead now. Let's find them, [player name].

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This ends here, Metzli. No more running.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Running? What do you think I'm running from? After all this time, I'm finally right where I need to be.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: And where's that exactly? Another Old One ruin? That's all this place is. There's no centre of the world here. Face it, Metzli, you've failed. Your Final Dawn has failed.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: The Final Dawn? Don't you see? There never was a Final Dawn. There never will be a Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You lied to the Emissaries? Why?

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Why? Quite simply because they let me.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You're mad!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Mad? Of course I'm mad! So many see madness as a weakness... As a burden. But to do what I do... To seek what I seek... Insanity is the only way one can ever walk that path.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: The people of this land... Their lives are built upon the truths presented to them. Ralos... Ranul... They put their faith in the gods they are told to believe in.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: From their first cries to their dying breaths, every choice they make is built upon those foundations. Foundations that have become so ingrained, that no sane person would ever question them.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: You have some, like the Emissaries, who dance on the edge of madness, but never leap into its embrace.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: It was never truth that they sought. They just took comfort in someone telling them what they wanted to hear.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: And so it falls to me. We stand here in Mokhaiotl, the last archive of the Old Ones. When their doom befell them, they did not pray, they preserved.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: They housed their collective knowledge, their collective power, right here. For years it's sat waiting... Waiting for a worthy inheritor. Waiting... for me.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Here, today, I will shatter the foundations that the sane never question. I will uncover the realities they shy away from. And in my madness, I will finally deliver that which the world has forgotten.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Today, I will be the one to finally find the truth.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Time to finish this.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli won't get far now she's injured. Let's find her.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It's over, Metzli. Surrender.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Never! I've fought too hard for this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look around, Metzli. This place is empty. Whatever 'truth' you're looking for, it's clearly not here.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: No! I won't let you stop me now!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: She's injured. She won't be going far. Let's get after her.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: There's... nothing here.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Of course there's nothing here, Metzli. What were you expecting? Did you think Ralos himself was going to pop out and share the secrets of the world with you?

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: But the Old Ones... This was their archive... Their gift...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: And yet it's empty. Perhaps this 'gift' was never meant for us. Or maybe it never existed at all. Either way, there's no 'truth' to be found here. Looks like we'll just have to keep making our own.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Metzli, you traitor!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Furia... It's over.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Over? No...

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Now it's over.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well... I suppose that's that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We'd better take Furia here to my mother. She'll be the one who decides what happens to her.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Furia, come with us, and don't even think about trying anything.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Are you coming, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You go on ahead. I want to take a look around first. I'll catch up with you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Fair enough. Don't take too long.

sections.title: Talking again if inventory was full

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. As I was saying, it feels only right for me to show my thanks to you by giving you a little something.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name].

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: So it's done?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It's done. Metzli is dead. This one here is all that remains.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Take her to the capital. Find a nice cell for her where she can await her fate.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: If I may, mother... Metzli and Ennius are already dead. The Emissaries are now without leaders and are unlikely to be much of a threat.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But there are still many out there who supported them. As far as they're concerned, we've already made two martyrs today. It might be prudent for us to keep Furia alive.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: My son is showing a remarkable amount of restraint and foresight. The cultists didn't replace him with an imposter, did they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I think it's definitely him.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then consider me impressed. We might make a king out of you yet.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Thank you, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Now, I must return to the capital. I need to see to arrangements around a new Teokan. [player name]. Itzla. Well done. Timoiva for now, and may the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, that was quite the adventure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, nice of you to show up right at the end. Trust you to skip all the hard work.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Tetamo! I was working very hard, thank you very much. I was conducting essential research on our enemy from the last place they'd expect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that so? Well, I'm sure you made many useful discoveries.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Indeed I did, iknami. Speaking of which, did you discover anything of note down in the ruins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes and no. It seems Metzli was right in a way. Mokhaiotl was meant to be an Old One archive. Thing is, they never finished it. Something killed the ones working on it while it was still being built.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Interesting... All this time we've thought of Mokhaiotl as the centre of the world, and yet it was something else entirely. Makes you wonder what else it is that we don't know...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Still, I guess it doesn't really matter. Metzli fooled herself into thinking that she could find all the answers, yet the beauty of this world is that we are free to look upon it as we desire.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But enough about Metzli. I have something far more important to talk about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: While I know you of course needed no convincing to put your life at risk to rescue me, it feels only right to show my thanks by giving you a little something.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, but I see you don't have space for it right now. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This blade once belonged to my grandfather, but I believe its path now lies with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Itzla, that's your sword!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: And I can't think of anyone more deserving to wield it, iknami. Now, I'd best return to the palace. Timoiva, my friend, and may the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Itzla.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, now that the local cult of murderers is no longer trying to kill me or my father, I'd say things are looking up.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm also finding I've got a bit more time on my hands now that things are all sorted. I suggested to mother I spend that time in the Colosseum. I don't know if she was totally convinced.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't imagine why...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, I won't keep you, iknami. I'm sure you've got plenty more adventures to go on. Timoiva, my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't happen to have another sword do you?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, iknami, as luck would have it, someone came along trying to sell a sword that perfectly matches the one I gave you. He claimed he just found it on the ground!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How odd...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Indeed. I'm sure it can't be the same one. After all, that was a one of a kind family heirloom, so you'd definitely have taken good care of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... definitely...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, I had to pay 250,000 coins to buy this one, so if you want it, that's how much I'll have to charge you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I don't have enough. I'll be back shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Use it well, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: As you wish, iknami.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm afraid not, iknami. You'll have to go and find where you left the one I gave you. I'm sure you won't have lost it. After it, it's a one of a kind family heirloom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... yes... I'll go see if I can find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Timoiva, my friend.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. It is good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Likewise, my Queen. How have things been since the Emissaries were dealt with?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Well, with the cult out of the way, we've been able to refocus on the recent opening of the kingdom. Signing the treaties was only the first step.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: There are new opportunities for us in areas such as trade that we're still yet to fully explore.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then of course you have Kourend. The complicated history between our kingdoms means we are yet to sign a treaty with them, but my hope is that will change soon.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then of course you have Kourend. The complicated history between our kingdoms means we are yet to sign a treaty with them.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: I was hoping to arrange one with their new king, but his reign turned out to be a rather short one. I must confess, I find the continued instability up there to be concerning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not the only one...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: We will have to see how their new king fares. He is young and inexperienced, but he may still have potential...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, if there's nothing else, I'm afraid I must return to my work. May the sun light your way, [player name].

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. It is good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Likewise, Servius. How are things?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Well, it is relieving to know that my life is no longer in danger, but there is no shortage of work to be done.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Sect of Ranul are planning a special Gathering to select their new Teokan, and we in the Church of Ralos have an important part to play. There is a lot to prepare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Given what happened in the crypt, I'm guessing things will be a bit different this time as well.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Indeed. For a start, the doors within the crypt seem to be beyond repair, meaning the ceremony will need some substantial changes.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: It does also mean that anyone can now access the caverns below, which reminds me, Itzla told me what you found down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Mokhaiotl. Not quite the centre of the world after all.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Well, I'd be careful of making assumptions, my child. Itzla said the ruins were meant to be an archive. A centre point for all the knowledge of the Old Ones.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: I'd say it makes perfect sense that they'd choose to name a place like that after Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I hadn't considered that possibility.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Well, being the Teokan of Ralos, I do have the odd moment of wisdom from time to time. Now, I must get back to my work. Timoiva, my child, and may the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Furia?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. I hear that cultist business of yours ended up getting sorted. Your Queen traded Teumo with the Highlord in return for a new mineral deal.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: On that topic, I'm going to guess that they didn't end up finding the centre of the world?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly, though they did find something. It seems that Mokhaiotl was actually meant to be an Old One archive, though it was never finished.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Ah, so once again you humans managed to misinterpret something the Old Ones left behind. Some things never change.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Anyway, was there something you wanted?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things?

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Well, things were quiet for a while, but then you dug up that Teumo business. The Highlord and the Highseer were both rather unhappy. The Highseer in particular. She now needs to find a new advisor.

lines.speaker: Attala
lines.text: Still, at least the world didn't end. Hard to imagine that some of you humans actually considered that a possibility! Your kind never change.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Welcome back, iknil. Were you able to deal with those interlopers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. They won't be a problem again.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: That is good to hear. The Moons of Peril already pose so great a threat. Neypotzli does not need any more dangers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you'd want to know as well that they did find Mokhaiotl. It turns out it was meant to be an archive.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: An archive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somewhere to house the collective knowledge of the Old Ones. Thing is, it was never finished. Something happened that stopped them from doing so.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: I see... The masters always hoped that humans and dwarves would be their inheritors. Mokhaiotl must have been part of that. To hear that it never came to be is... unfortunate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, Eyatlalli.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It is alright, iknil. Time is like a river. You can try to change the current, but in the end, it always reaches the sea. Some things are simply not meant to be.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Now, let us speak of something else.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes, iknil?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you about your masters.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: What do you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess anything really. I still know so little about them.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: If your hope is that I can tell you about a whole race, then I am likely to disappoint you, iknil. The masters were secretive individuals. They rarely divulged information that I did not require.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: All I can tell you about are the three individuals that I knew personally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's still something. What were they like?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: They were kind, but filled with sadness. As you know, they summoned me after another of their kind unleashed the Moons of Peril.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It was a dark time. Their people had suffered great calamity years earlier and their extinction was inevitable.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It seems that many, such as the shaman who summoned the Moons, felt that the only salvation left was destruction.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: My masters, however, felt differently. They fought for a world that was no longer their own. They believed in preserving these lands for those who would inhabit them afterwards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Us?

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: Yes. Humans and dwarves were recent arrivals here, but the masters saw you as inheritors. I believe in some ways, you offered them hope in a time filled with pain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Than you for sharing that with me.

lines.speaker: Eyatlalli
lines.text: It is not a problem, iknil.

lines.speaker: Emissary
lines.text: Another group left earlier. Without the Augur to guide them, they are lost.

lines.speaker: Emissary
lines.text: The Augur may be gone, but we remain. We must stay true to her teachings.

lines.speaker: Emissary
lines.text: I can't believe the Augur is gone. I really thought she would lead us to the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Emissary
lines.text: We become more fractured by the day. Earlier I heard someone claiming that Ennius and Furia weren't actually Ralos and Ranul reborn at all!
